---
title: Investigating the clustering of the VZ and SVZ cells and determining cell markers
date: 2024-05-09
metadata-files: 
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
---

## Goal of this analysis

1. Check whether the VZ and SVZ cells are clustering together and in a cluster that makes sense.
2. Determine markers that differentiate VZ and SVZ cells, as well as RL (VZ+SVZ) markers
3. Check the cell type markers we have right now and how much they overlap with the top markers from the differential gene analysis

## Files and directories

### Input

Hendrikse dataset can be found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/data/Aldinger_2021/glutamatergic_dev_Liam.RDS`

Original CCA integration file can be found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240506/cca/20240506_cca_integ.qs`

### Output

Results can be found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240509/cca`

## Process and Results

### Identifying the VZ and SVZ Cells

Firstly, lets load the datasets.
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(Seurat)
library(qs)
```
```{r eval=FALSE}
library(presto)
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
# Hendrikse dataset
hendrikse_srat <- readRDS("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/data/Aldinger_2021/glutamatergic_dev_Liam.RDS")

# Integrated dataset
integ_cca <- qread("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240506/cca/20240506_cca_integ.qs")
```

The Hendrikse dataset is just the rhombic lip portion of the Aldinger dataset. It had reannotated RL cells to differenciate between GCPs, VZs, and SVZs. Those labels need to be transfered over as they are not in the integrated dataset.

Firstly, I will extract the cell ids (rownames) of the VZ and SVZ cells separately.
```{r eval=FALSE}
# Cell ids for SVZ and VZ
svz <- rownames(hendrikse_srat@meta.data[hendrikse_srat@meta.data$new_cell_type == "RL-SVZ", ])
write.csv(svz, "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240509/cca/svz_cells.csv")

vz <- rownames(hendrikse_srat@meta.data[hendrikse_srat@meta.data$new_cell_type == "RL-VZ", ])
write.csv(vz, "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240509/cca/vz_cells.csv")

# As a sanity check, make sure the rownames exist with the integrated dataset
svz_check <- all(svz %in% rownames(integ_cca@meta.data))
svz_check 

vz_check <- all(vz %in% rownames(integ_cca@meta.data))
vz_check 
```

The both sanity checks are TRUE
```{r eval=FALSE}
[1] TRUE
[1] TRUE
```

Now with the cell ids, we can make a DimPlot highlighting the SVZ and VZ cells.
```{r eval=FALSE}

out_directory <- "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240509/cca"

highlight_cells <- list(svz = svz, vz = vz)
highlight_colours <- c("blue", "green")

# SVZ DimPlot
  svz_plt <- DimPlot(integ_cca, reduction = "umap", group.by = "snn_res.0.4", cells.highlight = highlight_cells, 
                    cols.highlight = highlight_colours, label = TRUE, label.size = 3, raster = FALSE) + 
      ggtitle("SVZ and VZ Cells")
    filename <- "CCA_0.4_integ_SVZ_VZ.png"
    ggsave(filename = filename, plot = svz_plt, path = out_directory, 
            width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 600)
```

![It appears that the VZ and SVZ cells are clustering closely together which is a good sign. They both are in Cluster 7](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240509/cca/CCA_0.4_integ_SVZ_VZ.png)


### Find VZ vs SVZ Markers

Lets find the markers that differentiate SVZ from VZ
```{r eval=FALSE}
DefaultAssay(integ_cca) <- "RNA"
svz_markers <- FindMarkers(object = integ_cca, ident.1 = svz, ident.2 = vz)
head(svz_markers, 20)
write.csv(svz_markers, "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240509/cca/svz_vz_markers.csv")
```

The top 20 SVZ markers comparing to VZ using all genes
```{r eval=FALSE}
                    p_val avg_log2FC pct.1 pct.2    p_val_adj
SLC35F3      3.720111e-83   3.269900 0.906 0.279 1.774530e-78
KAZN         1.379777e-71  -2.908006 0.215 0.858 6.581673e-67
CNTNAP2      4.270437e-70   2.215331 0.961 0.781 2.037041e-65
WLS          1.184624e-59  -2.433426 0.192 0.814 5.650774e-55
NCKAP5       2.472250e-58  -1.669816 0.811 0.989 1.179288e-53
SEZ6L        1.769396e-57   2.191449 0.886 0.415 8.440194e-53
SLC22A3      3.368169e-55  -2.631250 0.023 0.609 1.606650e-50
ASIC2        1.109056e-53   3.566673 0.726 0.208 5.290306e-49
GPC6         8.537977e-53   2.274550 0.844 0.456 4.072700e-48
DPYD         1.124536e-50   2.842492 0.651 0.131 5.364151e-46
PTPRM        1.822125e-50  -2.090709 0.270 0.806 8.691718e-46
SLIT2        4.939418e-49  -1.796267 0.808 0.984 2.356152e-44
RP11-820L6.1 4.950947e-49  -2.059303 0.225 0.765 2.361651e-44
ADCY2        5.246335e-49  -2.341649 0.081 0.639 2.502554e-44
NTRK2        8.822774e-49  -2.251610 0.228 0.743 4.208551e-44
EBF1         3.667164e-48   2.131189 0.779 0.352 1.749274e-43
PTPRT        2.516439e-42  -2.578719 0.134 0.628 1.200367e-37
KCNMA1       6.562979e-42  -1.871231 0.417 0.833 3.130606e-37
TRPM3        7.183934e-42   1.923865 0.915 0.686 3.426808e-37
NRG3         2.566056e-40  -1.655311 0.524 0.863 1.224034e-35
```

### Find RL Markers

Now lets find the markers that differentiate RL (SVZ and VZ) from all other cells.
```{r eval=FALSE}
rl <- c(svz, vz)
rl_markers <- FindMarkers(object = integ_cca, ident.1 = rl)
head(rl_markers, 20)
write.csv(rl_markers, "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240509/cca/rl_markers.csv")
```

The top 20 RL markers comparing to all other cells using all genes
```{r eval=FALSE}
              p_val avg_log2FC pct.1 pct.2 p_val_adj
MT-RNR2           0  1.7766599 0.652 0.074         0
KIAA0368          0  0.9659021 0.257 0.022         0
NPAS3             0  2.0584765 0.652 0.137         0
KALRN             0  2.0906336 0.741 0.184         0
LINC00693         0  1.4556258 0.251 0.011         0
MT-RNR1           0  2.0732470 0.588 0.056         0
RORA              0  2.2865661 0.871 0.227         0
FAM155A           0  1.5957783 0.550 0.079         0
KIAA2022          0  1.3170235 0.342 0.033         0
RNA28S5           0  2.6945029 0.750 0.059         0
RP11-649A16.1     0  3.6517960 0.967 0.075         0
ILDR2             0  1.5694302 0.342 0.035         0
LPPR1             0  1.8260298 0.438 0.044         0
SUV420H1          0  1.1340695 0.303 0.028         0
LARGE             0  1.5129783 0.391 0.039         0
TRPM3             0  3.4418560 0.790 0.163         0
CASC15            0  2.7586174 0.918 0.213         0
RP11-444D3.1      0  1.1139796 0.245 0.019         0
SKIV2L2           0  1.0385699 0.263 0.021         0
TENM4             0  2.4995011 0.545 0.068         0
```

TRPM3 appears to be a good candidate for RL marker. This paper also shows high TRPM3 expression RL cells: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9886277/

### Find how our cell type markers perform in the differential gene analysis

Most of our cell type markers are not in the 4902 more variable features. A variable feature for integration and a cell type marker does not necessarily have any correlation, however, this can be its own separate investigation. Right now, we will perform the differential gene analysis using orthologous genes only.

First lets resubset the integrated dataset to only have the orthologous genes and find the top orthologous markers for each cluster.
```{r eval=FALSE}
ortho_csv <- read.csv("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/scrnaseq_Leo/utilities/20240402_hgnc_mgi_orth_genes.csv")

ortho_integ <- subset(integ_cca, features = ortho_csv$HGNC.symbol)

ortho_integ$snn_res.0.4 <- fct_relevel(ortho_integ$snn_res.0.4, str_sort(levels(ortho_integ$snn_res.0.4), numeric = TRUE))

ortho_markers <- wilcoxauc(
  ortho_integ,
  group_by = "snn_res.0.4",
  assay = "data",
  seurat_assay = "RNA"
)

write.csv(ortho_markers, file = "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240509/cca/ortho_markers.csv")
top_markers <- top_markers(ortho_markers, n = 3000)
write.csv(top_markers, file = "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240509/cca/top_ortho_markers.csv")
```

Note: presto::top_markers() uses auc (area under receiver operator curve) to rank markers.

Now lets determine where markers rank for each cluster.
```{r}
top_markers <- read.csv("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240509/cca/top_ortho_markers.csv")
cell_markers <- read.csv("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/scrnaseq_Leo/utilities/cell_gene_mapping.csv")
ortho_markers <- read.csv("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240509/cca/ortho_markers.csv")

result_df <- data.frame(cluster = integer(),
                         gene = character(),
                         rank = integer(),
                         pval = integer(),
                         padj = integer(),
                         logFC = integer(),
                         avgExpr = integer(),
                         statistic = integer(),
                         auc = integer(),
                         pct_in = integer(),
                         pct_out = integer(),
                         cell_type = character(),
                         stringsAsFactors = FALSE)


for (i in 1:nrow(cell_markers)) {
  gene <- cell_markers$gene[i]
  for (j in 3:ncol(top_markers)) {
    # Get the cluster name
    cluster_name <- colnames(top_markers)[j]
    
    # Check if the gene is present in the current cluster
    if (gene %in% top_markers[[cluster_name]]) {
      # Find the rank of the gene within the cluster
      gene_rank <- which(top_markers[[cluster_name]] == gene)
      # Print the result
      cluster <- as.integer(substr(cluster_name, 2, nchar(cluster_name)))
      ortho_marker_subset <- ortho_markers[ortho_markers$feature == gene & ortho_markers$group == cluster, ]
      pval <- ortho_marker_subset$pval
      padj <- ortho_marker_subset$padj
      logFC <- ortho_marker_subset$logFC
      avgExpr <- ortho_marker_subset$avgExpr
      statistic <- ortho_marker_subset$statistic
      auc <- ortho_marker_subset$auc
      pct_in <- ortho_marker_subset$pct_in
      pct_out <- ortho_marker_subset$pct_out
      result_df <- rbind(result_df, data.frame(cluster = cluster,
                                               gene = gene,
                                               rank = gene_rank,
                                               pval = pval,
                                               padj = padj,
                                               logFC = logFC,
                                               avgExpr = avgExpr,
                                              statistic =  statistic,
                                                auc = auc,
                                                pct_in = pct_in,
                                                pct_out = pct_out,
                                               cell_type = cell_markers$lineage[i]))
    }
  }
}
result_df <- result_df %>%
  arrange(cluster, rank) %>%
  group_by(cluster, rank)
head(result_df, 20)
write.csv(result_df, "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240509/cca/marker_overlap.csv")
```

Lets also take a look at where the RL markers rank
```{r}
top_markers <- read.csv("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240509/cca/top_ortho_markers.csv")
rl_markers <- read.csv("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240509/cca/rl_markers.csv")
ortho_markers <- read.csv("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240509/cca/ortho_markers.csv")

rl_markers <- rl_markers %>%
  arrange(p_val, desc(avg_log2FC)) 

rl_markers <- head(rl_markers, 100)

rl_markers

result_rl_df <- data.frame(cluster = integer(),
                          gene = character(),
                          rank = integer(),
                          pval = integer(),
                          padj = integer(),
                          logFC = integer(),
                          pct_1 = integer(),
                          pct_2 = integer(),
                          stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

for (i in 1:nrow(rl_markers)) {
  gene <- rl_markers$X[i]
  for (j in 3:ncol(top_markers)) {
    # Get the cluster name
    cluster_name <- colnames(top_markers)[j]
    
    # Check if the gene is present in the current cluster
    if (gene %in% top_markers[[cluster_name]]) {
      # Find the rank of the gene within the cluster
      gene_rank <- which(top_markers[[cluster_name]] == gene)
      # Print the result
      cluster <- as.integer(substr(cluster_name, 2, nchar(cluster_name)))
      pval <- rl_markers$p_val[i]
      padj <- rl_markers$p_val_adj[i]
      logFC <- rl_markers$avg_log2FC[i]
      pct_1 <- rl_markers$pct.1[i]
      pct_2 <- rl_markers$pct.2[i]
      result_rl_df <- rbind(result_rl_df, data.frame(cluster = cluster,
                                                    gene = gene,
                                                    rank = gene_rank,
                                                    pval = pval,
                                                    padj = padj,
                                                    logFC = logFC,
                                                    pct_1 = pct_1,
                                                    pct_2 = pct_2))
    }
  }
}
result_rl_df <- result_rl_df %>%
  arrange(cluster, rank) %>%
  group_by(cluster, rank)
head(result_rl_df, 20)
write.csv(result_rl_df, "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240509/cca/rl_marker_overlap.csv")
```

```{r}
print(sessionInfo())
```
